key_insight: Doeblin curves generalize Doeblin coefficients into a nonlinear, level-dependent contraction map that can certify information contraction even when the usual coefficient is zero.
engineering_relevance: This gives sharper guarantees for noisy optimization, reliable computation under circuit noise, and differential privacy analysis in iterative algorithms.
method: Introduce a nonlinear Doeblin curve to quantify contraction over sets of input distributions at specified divergence and power levels.
Derive a new variational characterization of Doeblin coefficients and use it to bound the proposed curves.
Develop power-constrained variants and apply them to generalization, error, and privacy bounds in broader settings.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical upper and lower bounds and multiple application case studies, but does not mention experiments or datasets.
watch_item: The guarantees are still theory-driven and may require careful estimation or verification of kernel-specific divergence parameters in practice.
